profile.name: Alex Morgan
profile.bio: eng @stripe • prev @googlex @deepmind • shipping bits since forever • opinions my own

recent_tweets.text: Fascinating paper on consensus algorithms in geo-distributed systems. The latency trade-offs with Paxos vs Raft are still underappreciated by most engineers.
Hot take: most 'AI-native' startups are just wrapper companies with good branding. The ones that survive will be those who own the data layer.
Just pushed a Rust-based stream processor that handles 2M events/sec on a single node. OSS soon. If you're still using Kafka for <10M events/day you might be overengineering.
Reminder that code review is a communication skill, not a gatekeeping tool. If your team dreads reviews, the process is broken, not the code.
